note: Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/CrossProduct.lean:110-114 — theorem cross_dot_cross (u v w x : Fin 3 → R): u⨯₃v ⬝ᵥ w⨯₃x = u⬝ᵥw*v⬝ᵥx - u⬝ᵥx*v⬝ᵥw, docstring 'the scalar quadruple product identity, related to the Binet-Cauchy identity'. Setting w=u,x=v recovers Lagrange's identity |u×v|²=|u|²|v|²-(u·v)² (used exactly this way in Mathlib/Geometry/Euclidean/Angle/Unoriented/CrossProduct.lean:32-37 as an inline step, not a standalone lemma); the cited decl is the strictly more general 4-argument Binet-Cauchy form, and only in R^3 (not the general n-dim sum-over-pairs identity).

Mathlib/Logic/Relation.lean:896-897 — theorem church_rosser (h : ∀ a b c, r a b → r a c → ∃ d, ReflGen r b d ∧ ReflTransGen r c d) ...: Join (ReflTransGen r) b c, docstring 'A sufficient condition for the Church-Rosser property'. Mathlib's own docstring at GroupTheory/FreeGroup/Basic.lean:34-35 names the specialized FreeGroup.Red.church_rosser 'also known as Newman's diamond lemma' (repeated in the file's Tags). Caveat: the hypothesis used is one-step strong local confluence (no well-foundedness/termination premise), not the classical Newman's-Lemma hypothesis of local confluence + termination on a general ARS — same topic, distinct sufficient condition, so graded related rather than exact.

Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Basic.lean:150-152 — def completeBipartiteGraph (V W : Type*) : SimpleGraph (V ⊕ W) where Adj v w := v.isLeft ∧ w.isRight ∨ v.isRight ∧ w.isLeft. The utility graph K_{3,3} is exactly completeBipartiteGraph (Fin 3) (Fin 3); Mathlib only has the fully generic V,W construction, no named K_{3,3}/utility-graph instance.
